Topic sections
Cardiology Block — Weeks 1–5
Week 1

Cardiology — Pharmacology, Physiology & Anatomy

HF Medications Autonomic Pharm Cardiac Physiology Action Potential ECG Basics Cardiac Anatomy
HF drug mortality, ACEi/ARB/statin side effects, anticholinergic vs. sympathomimetic toxidromes, heart sounds, PV loops, cardiac action potential, SA node automaticity, ECG interpretation, and pericardial anatomy.
93 questions ›
Week 2

Cardiology — Valvular Disease, Cardiac Imaging & Pericarditis

Aortic Stenosis Aortic Regurgitation Mitral Stenosis Mitral Regurgitation Cardiac Imaging Pericarditis Endocarditis
Heart murmurs, valvular disease diagnosis and management, cardiac imaging modalities (echo, CT, MRI, cath), acute pericarditis, rheumatic fever, endocarditis, and valve replacement options.
108 questions ›
Week 3

Cardiology — MI, ACS, CABG, PAD & Aortic Disease

STEMI/NSTEMI Fibrinolysis Post-MI Complications CABG Peripheral Arterial Disease Aortic Aneurysm Cardiac Rehab
STEMI/NSTEMI pathophysiology, ECG localization, fibrinolytic therapy, post-MI complications (VSR, papillary rupture, aneurysm), CABG indications, PAD management, AAA screening and repair, and cardiac rehabilitation.
68 questions ›
Week 4

Cardiology — Heart Failure, Hypertension & Pediatric Cardiology

HF Staging/Classification HFrEF/HFpEF HF Medications LVAD & Transplant Hypertension Congenital Heart Disease Kawasaki Disease
ACC/AHA staging, NYHA classification, HFrEF vs. HFpEF, ARNI/SGLT2/ivabradine mechanisms, LVAD complications, cardiac transplant, hypertension management, pediatric CHD (ToF, VSD, TAPVR), and Kawasaki disease.
82 questions ›
Week 5

Cardiology — Arrhythmias, Syncope & Antiarrhythmic Pharmacology

Bradyarrhythmias AV Block Syncope SVT/VT/AF Atrial Flutter CHADS2 Score Antiarrhythmic Drugs
Sinus node dysfunction, AV block classification, syncope (vasovagal vs. cardiac), SVT, wide complex tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, CHADS2 anticoagulation, cardioversion, adenosine, amiodarone, and cholinergic/adrenergic pharmacology.
72 questions ›
Respirology & ENT Block — Weeks 6–9
Week 6

Respirology — COPD, Asthma, Pleural Anatomy & Gas Exchange

COPD Management Asthma Pharmacology Pleural Anatomy V/Q & Gas Exchange PFT Interpretation Oxygen-Hgb Curve Chest X-Ray
COPD and asthma management step-up, pleural anatomy and thoracocentesis, laryngeal muscles, V/Q mismatch, hypoxemia mechanisms, alveolar gas equation, A-a gradient, PFT patterns, surfactant, and air bronchograms.
84 questions ›
Week 7

Respirology — Pneumonia, CF, ILD, ABG Interpretation & Chemoreceptors

Nosocomial Pneumonia Cystic Fibrosis IPF & Sarcoidosis ABG Interpretation Chemoreceptors Asthma Control COPD vs Asthma
Nosocomial vs. CAP, CF genetics and complications, IPF and sarcoidosis, ABG interpretation (respiratory acidosis/alkalosis, A-a gradient), central vs. peripheral chemoreceptors, asthma control criteria, and COPD management step-up.
78 questions ›
Week 8

Respirology — Hemoptysis, Lung Cancer, Epistaxis & Pediatric Asthma

Hemoptysis Lung Nodules & TNM Epistaxis Pediatric Asthma Cystic Fibrosis Pleural Effusions Trikafta
Hemoptysis mechanisms and management, lung nodule risk stratification and TNM staging, epistaxis anatomy and treatment, pediatric asthma (API, preschool diagnosis, management by age), Trikafta mechanism, and pleural effusion classification (Light's criteria).
56 questions ›
Week 9

ENT — Salivary Glands, Head & Neck Cancer, Neck Anatomy & Larynx

Salivary Glands Head & Neck Cancer / HPV Neck Fasciae Carotid Sheath Laryngeal Pathology Esophageal Motility Pediatric Airway Thyroid Nodules
Salivary gland infections and tumours, HPV+ oropharyngeal cancer, neck fasciae and spaces, carotid sheath anatomy, laryngeal muscles and pathology, achalasia and esophageal motility disorders, dysphagia phases, pediatric stridor and foreign body aspiration, and thyroid nodule workup.
70 questions ›
Gastrointestinal Block — Weeks 10–13
Week 10

GI — Physiology, Anatomy, GERD, Peptic Ulcer & Antiemetics

GI Physiology & Motility GI Hormones Abdominal Wall & Hernias GERD & PUD H. pylori Antiemetics Gastroparesis
GI wall layers and plexuses, ICCs, motility patterns, CCK/gastrin/serotonin, embryology and blood supply, abdominal wall layers, umbilical folds, inguinal hernias, GERD diagnosis and treatment, peptic ulcer disease (HOPP complications), H. pylori eradication, antiemetic pharmacology, and gastroparesis management.
109 questions ›
Week 11

GI Block — Upper GI, IBD & Colorectal

GI Cancers CRC Screening IBD GI Bleeding Celiac Diarrhea IBS
Module questions, Weekly Quiz, Meds2024, and New Questions covering upper GI disease, IBD, colorectal cancer screening, and more.
137 questions ›
Week 12

GI Block — Liver, Biliary & Pancreas

Hepatitis Cirrhosis Jaundice Pancreatitis Gallstones HCC Liver Failure
Liver function tests, viral hepatitis, portal hypertension, biliary anatomy, acute and chronic pancreatitis, and hepatic neoplasms.
105 questions ›
Week 13

GI Block — Surgical Abdomen & Peds GI

Bowel Obstruction Appendicitis Diverticulitis Ischemia Anorectal Peds GI
Acute abdomen, bowel obstruction, appendicitis, diverticulitis, mesenteric ischemia, hemorrhoids, and pediatric GI including constipation.
78 questions ›
Nephrology & Urology Block — Weeks 14–17
Week 14

Nephrology — Fluids, Electrolytes & Acid-Base

House's Law Na/K Disorders Acid-Base ABG Interpretation Nephron Anatomy Diuretics IV Fluids
Sodium and water handling, potassium disorders, anion gap, ABG interpretation, RAAS, ADH, nephron physiology, diuretic mechanisms, and renal anatomy.
91 questions ›
Week 15

Nephrology — AKI, CKD & Glomerular Disease

AKI CKD Glomerulonephritis Nephrotic/Nephritic Transplant Diuretics
AKI classification, CKD staging, glomerular disease, nephrotic vs. nephritic syndrome, proteinuria, kidney transplantation, and diuretic pharmacology.
85 questions ›
Week 16

Urology — Scrotal, Renal Masses, BPH & Cancer

Testicular Cancer Torsion BPH Prostate Cancer Renal Masses Hematuria Peds Urology
Scrotal masses, testicular torsion, undescended testis, phimosis, enuresis, BPH/prostate cancer, renal cell carcinoma, urothelial cancer, and pediatric nephrology.
105 questions ›
Week 17

Urology & Renal — Incontinence, Stones & Dialysis

Incontinence Kidney Stones Dialysis Transplantation Voiding Symptoms
Urinary incontinence types and treatment, kidney stone types and management, dialysis access and complications, and renal transplantation.
44 questions ›

How to use these quizzes

  • This is a single self-contained file — all 17 quizzes are bundled in. No other files needed.
  • Click any week card to open that quiz inline. Use Back to Hub to return.
  • Use the section filter buttons at the top of each quiz to drill into Module Questions, Weekly Quiz, Meds2024, or New Questions.
  • Hit Shuffle before each session to randomize question order and avoid pattern memorization.
  • After completing a quiz, note your percentage and any missed questions — then use the filter to retake only that section.
PoM 1 · Schulich School of Medicine & Dentistry  ·  Generated from the GI and GU Question Banks
